title: Hungary Political Transition Update — Apr 18, 2026
summary: Three gap days were backfilled: the April 12 election landslide (Tisza 53.6%, supermajority of 138 seats, Orbán concedes), April 15 government formation announcement (new government by early May, Magyar demands president resign), and April 16 EU delegation arrival in Budapest to negotiate €17 billion in frozen funds. KPIs updated with election results and EU fund figures. Timeline and map points expanded with key political locations. Hungary's transition remains active with government transfer expected by May 12.

title: Breaking: Analysis examines whether Hungary under its new leader can realistically end dep
summary: Analysis examines whether Hungary under its new leader can realistically end dependence on Russian energy as promised

title: Hungary Political Transition Update — Thu Apr 16, 2026
summary: A high-level European Commission delegation arrived in Budapest for urgent talks with Péter Magyar's transition team to negotiate the release of approximately €17 billion in frozen EU funds.

title: Hungary Political Transition Update — Wed Apr 15, 2026
summary: Péter Magyar announced that Hungary's new government could assume power at the beginning of May following President Tamás Sulyok's assurance that Magyar would lead government formation.

title: Breaking: Analysis of Hungary's populist politics following Orbán's electoral defeat.
summary: Analysis of Hungary's populist politics following Orbán's electoral defeat.

title: Breaking: Orbán Swept from Power After 16 Years
summary: Viktor Orbán swept from power after 16 years ruling Hungary, ending his authoritarian hold on the EU/NATO member state.

title: Hungary Political Transition Update — Sun Apr 12, 2026
summary: Péter Magyar's Tisza Party won Hungary's parliamentary election with 53.6% of the vote and 138 of 199 seats — a two-thirds supermajority enabling constitutional amendments.
